submitting_agency_information.from_public_agency: City of Santa Rosa Planning and Economic Development Department

project_information.project_title: West College Avenue Pedestrian Safety and Streetscape Improvements Project
project_information.project_applicant: City of Santa Rosa Public Works Department
project_information.project_location_specific: West College Avenue between Dutton Avenue and Stony Point Road, including intersections at Dutton Avenue, Rutledge Avenue, and Stony Point Road, Santa Rosa, CA 95401
project_information.description_of_nature_purpose_and_benefi: The project will construct minor street and sidewalk improvements along approximately 0.8 miles of West College Avenue, including installation of new ADA-compliant curb ramps, high-visibility crosswalk striping, pedestrian refuge islands within the existing paved right-of-way, upgraded pedestrian-scale lighting at key crossings, minor pavement grinding and overlay to restore disturbed areas, and replacement of damaged sidewalk panels and uplifted sections caused by tree roots; all work occurs within the existing public street and sidewalk footprint with no expansion of the roadway or additional travel lanes.

The purpose of the project is to improve pedestrian safety and accessibility for residents, school children walking to nearby Santa Rosa High School and adjacent elementary schools, transit riders accessing existing bus stops, and nearby small businesses and multifamily housing residents; beneficiaries include local residents (many of whom are low- to moderate-income renters), people with disabilities who currently struggle to navigate broken sidewalks and non-compliant ramps, older adults who rely on walking and public transit for errands and medical appointments, and drivers who will experience clearer lane markings and better organized crossings, reducing the likelihood of collisions and near-misses at unsignalized intersections.

agency_approvals_and_exemption_status.name_of_public_agency_approving_project: City of Santa Rosa City Council
agency_approvals_and_exemption_status.name_of_person_or_agency_carrying_out_pr: City of Santa Rosa Public Works Department, Capital Projects Engineering Division
agency_approvals_and_exemption_status.reasons_why_project_is_exempt_line_1: The project involves minor alterations to existing public streets, sidewalks, crosswalks, and related appurtenant facilities within the existing public right-of-way, including repair and replacement of damaged sidewalk panels, upgrades to existing curb ramps to meet current ADA standards, restriping of existing travel lanes and crosswalks, and installation of pedestrian refuge islands and lighting within already disturbed paved areas; there is no expansion of the roadway capacity, no increase in the number of travel lanes, and no extension of infrastructure into previously undeveloped or environmentally sensitive areas, so the work fits squarely within the scope of a Class 1 categorical exemption for existing facilities under CEQA Guidelines §15301(c).
agency_approvals_and_exemption_status.reasons_why_project_is_exempt_line_2: All construction activity will occur in a fully urbanized corridor that is already built out with residential, commercial, and institutional uses, and the project will not result in any significant effects relating to traffic, noise, air quality, or water quality beyond temporary and short-term construction-related impacts that are typical of routine street maintenance and minor safety improvements; there are no wetlands, riparian areas, or habitat for special-status species within or adjacent to the project limits, no work will occur on or near a listed historic resource, and none of the exceptions to the use of categorical exemptions under CEQA Guidelines §15300.2 apply, as the project area is not located on a hazardous waste site, does not contain unusual circumstances that could lead to a significant environmental effect, and will not cumulatively contribute to a significant impact when viewed in connection with other nearby projects.

applicant_filing_and_signature.title: Senior Planner, City of Santa Rosa Planning and Economic Development Department
